description:
Replaces the original Clerk plan. We're self-hosting **better-auth** on AWS so user identity stays inside the BAA boundary alongside MDI patient pointers — Clerk's BAA is Enterprise-tier only and would put a copy of identity data outside our boundary.

**Scope**
- Install better-auth and configure against our Postgres (Drizzle migrations land the `users`, `accounts`, `sessions`, `verifications` tables; later additions: `passkeys`, `two_factor`).
- Database-backed sessions (Postgres) with Redis read-through cache for hot lookups. JWT-only sessions are rejected — we need revocation-on-the-spot for compliance termination, password reset, and suspected fraud.
- Route handler at `src/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts` exposes better-auth.
- Thin facade at `src/lib/auth.ts` (`getSession`, `requireUser`) — **every** auth call goes through this facade. Lint-enforced. This is the swap-cost insulator.

**Out of scope (separate tickets)**
- Sign-up / sign-in UI → T-014
- Middleware + protected routes → T-015
- Patient profile + KMS-encrypted PII columns → T-016

**Decisions**
- Skip the better-auth Stripe plugin in v1 — wrong shape (we want to create the Stripe customer after consents, not at signup). Revisit in Phase F.
- No organizations plugin — Apoth is direct-to-patient.

**Acceptance**
- `src/lib/auth.ts` exists and is the only path callers use to reach better-auth.
- A test user can be created via the better-auth API, persisted in `users`, and a session row exists.
- Swapping the underlying lib would require changes only inside `src/lib/auth.ts` (verified by reviewing call sites).
title: Auth provider setup (better-auth, self-hosted)
